heaviest_rainfall.status: On 09 November 2025, a record rainfall of 47.89mm was measured, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  24.6mm per hour was recorded as falling at times during the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: On 09 November 2025, the rain lasted for 144.8 hours, which is the longest time recorded by our database.  47.89mm accumulated and it fell at 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The highest recorded number of lightning strikes in a single day is {highest_count} on {highest_date} since records began on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: No storms are expected.  The last storm was observed on 18 July 2026 and continued for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: This period, starting 03 July 2026, is ranked 1st among the longest dry periods since 23 September 2024.  It has lasted 22 days so far.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not a cold snap right now.  There have been 3 this year, compared to a usual average of 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature difference recorded was 27.7°C on 24 April 2026.  On that date the temperature reached 23.2°C and fell to -4.5°C.

heatwaves.status: A heatwave is active and has been ongoing for 24 days since 2026-07-01.  This year has seen 3 heatwaves, compared to an average of 1.9 per year.

tropical.status: The most recent tropical night was on 28 June 2026 and this has only occurred 68 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (215°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The largest wind gust ever was on 27 January 2026 with a value of 128.2 mph.

run.status: The top recorded wind run is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 4.8°C) and outside temperature (down 3.9°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 7%).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 1324 m), light levels (up 9899 lux), dew point temperature (up 1.9°C), and feels like temperature (up 6.0°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 739.1m lower than yesterday, 1323.9m lower than the past week, 968.2m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels for yesterday ranged from 1490m to 5530m and averaged 2850m, while the past week and month indicated warmer conditions.
cloud levels.summary: Expect mostly clear skies with high cloud.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures should be quite comfortable. Outside; heat will be very noticeable, though not extreme. Also, intensely sticky air and very little to no wind will be present. A few light raindrops may fall, mostly clear skies with high cloud, and sunlight will be very intense and bright. Weather patterns to stay the same.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
